entries.main_competence: 1.0  Appreciate   different implications and layers of meaning in literary works
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Apply critical thinking when reading, discussing and writing about literary texts
entries.learning_activity: (a)  Analyse themes depicted in selected plays
teaching_learning_activities: Engagement with a text: Guide students in manageable groups through reading, analysing and discussing the depicted themes in a selected play
Discussion: Guide students in manageable groups through discussing themes depicted in selected plays
entries.assessment: Themes  depicted in selected plays are well analysed
resources: Selected literary texts and audio/audio-visual materials
entries.references: TIE, Literature in English for Secondary Schools Form Four: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Apply critical thinking when reading, discussing and writing about literary texts
entries.learning_activity: (c)  Relate personal experiences to themes in selected plays
teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorming: Guide students in manageable groups through brainstorming personal life experiences
Discussion: Guide students through relating personal life experiences to themes portrayed in selected plays
entries.assessment: Personal experiences are well related to themes in selected plays

entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Apply critical thinking when reading, discussing and writing about literary texts
entries.learning_activity: (d)  Discuss the  motivations of characters’ choices and decisions in selected plays
teaching_learning_activities: Engagement with a text: Guide students in manageable groups through discussing intrinsic and extrinsic reasons for character’s behaviors, choices and decisions in selected plays
entries.assessment: Motivations of characters’ choices and decisions in selected plays are well discussed

entries.main_competence: 1.0  Appreciate   different implications and layers of meaning in literary works
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Apply critical thinking when reading, discussing and writing about literary texts
entries.learning_activity: (e)  Suggest solutions to challenges depicted in selected plays
teaching_learning_activities: Dramatisation: Guide students through staging some scenes portraying challenges in a selected play

Discussion: Guide students through discussing and suggesting solutions to the depicted challenges by applying principles of resolution (affiliate, empathize, engage, own, self-restrain and build trust)
entries.assessment: Solutions to challenges depicted in selected plays are clearly suggested
entries.references: TIE, Literature in English for Secondary Schools Form Four: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Compose literary works
entries.specific_competence: 2.1 Synthesize literary characteristics, themes, and/or approaches in several literary texts
entries.learning_activity: (a)  Explain the concepts of character, plot, setting, stage direction and spectacle in a play
teaching_learning_activities: Discussion: Guide students in manageable groups through discussing the concepts of character, plot, setting, stage direction and spectacle
entries.assessment: The concepts of character, plot, setting, stage direction and spectacle are well explained
resources: Selected literary texts and audio/audio-visual materials
entries.references: TIE, Literature in English for Secondary Schools Form Four: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Compose literary works
entries.specific_competence: 2.1 Synthesize literary characteristics, themes, and/or approaches in several literary texts
entries.learning_activity: (b)  Analyse stages of plot development
teaching_learning_activities: Project activity: Task students to analyse stages of plot development a play of their choice (exposition, rising action, climax, falling action and resolution)
Exploration: Guide students in manageable groups through exploring stages of plot development
entries.assessment: Stages of plot development are correctly analysed
entries.references: TIE, Literature in English for Secondary Schools Form Four: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Compose literary works
entries.specific_competence: 2.1 Synthesize literary characteristics, themes, and/or approaches in several literary texts
entries.learning_activity: (c)  Apply literary skills to compose a two-acts play
teaching_learning_activities: Collaborative composition: Guide students in pairs or groups through composing a play
entries.assessment: Literary skills for composing a two-acts play are correctly applied
entries.references: TIE, Literature in English for Secondary Schools Form Four: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Compose literary works
entries.specific_competence: 2.2 Recognise ambiguity and uncertainty as necessary in creating literary works
entries.learning_activity: (a)  Discuss dramatic and situational irony in selected plays
teaching_learning_activities: Engagement with texts: Guide students through analysing congruities between what happens and what is expected; what is known to audience and what is known to characters
Discussion: Guide students through discussing dramatic and situational irony in selected plays
Explanation: Guide students through explaining the concepts of dramatic and situational ironies
entries.assessment: Dramatic and situational irony in selected plays are well discussed
entries.references: TIE, Literature in English for Secondary Schools Form Four: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Compose literary works
entries.specific_competence: 2.2 Recognise ambiguity and uncertainty as necessary in creating literary works
entries.learning_activity: (b)  Discuss the effectiveness of suspense in selected plays
teaching_learning_activities: Engagement with texts: Guide students in manageable groups through analysing how conflict, pacing, red herrings, atmosphere and high stakes are used in selected plays
Discussion: Guide students in manageable groups through discussing how suspense controls the story line in selected plays
entries.assessment: The effectiveness of suspense in selected plays is well discussed
entries.references: TIE, Literature in English for Secondary Schools Form Four: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 2.0 Compose literary works
entries.specific_competence: 2.2 Recognise ambiguity and uncertainty as necessary in creating literary works
entries.learning_activity: (c)  Explain how antithesis creates ambiguity in selected plays
teaching_learning_activities: Engagement with texts: Guide students through identifying exciting, apprehensive and uncertain conditions; disappointing ends; self-contradicting statements; and deliberate misleads in selected plays
Discussion: Guide students through discussing how antithesis creates ambiguity in selected plays
entries.assessment: Creation of ambiguity through antithesis in selected plays is well explained
entries.references: TIE, Literature in English for Secondary Schools Form Four: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 1.3    Create imaginative and original literary works
entries.specific_competence: 2.2 Recognise ambiguity and uncertainty as necessary in creating literary works
entries.learning_activity: (a) Make a plan for composing a play (plot, setting, characterisation and stage direction)
teaching_learning_activities: Shared planning: Collaborate with students to make a plan for composing a play
Collaborative planning: Guide students in pairs or manageable groups through making a plan for composing a play
entries.assessment: A plan for composing a play is correctly made
resources: Selected literary texts, ICT tools and audio/audio-visual materials
entries.references: TIE, Literature in English for Secondary Schools Form Four: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.main_competence: 1.3    Create imaginative and original literary works
entries.specific_competence: 2.2 Recognise ambiguity and uncertainty as necessary in creating literary works
entries.learning_activity: (c)  Apply ICT tools and digital platforms in writing and publishing a play
teaching_learning_activities: Skills lab: Guide students through composing a play using ICT tools
Publishing: Guide students independently or in manageable groups through publishing their plays on available digital platforms
entries.assessment: ICT tools and digital platforms are appropriately applied in writing and publishing a play
entries.references: TIE, Literature in English for Secondary Schools Form Four: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education
